LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF SALVATORE SARINELLI'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT, TO ENTER INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT ON SETTLEMENT, FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER & ISSUING OF RESTRAINING ORDERS - THE COURT INTERPRETED THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT INCLUDING THE ISSUE WITH RESPECT TO THE CAFE AND LIQUOR LICENSE. PARTIES ARE AGREEABLE TO THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECIEVER WITH LIMITED POWER. THE COURT INSTRUCTED THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER UNTIL MARCH 24, 2007. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE BY MARCH 27, 2008, BY A JOINT LETTER TO THE COURT, EACH PARTY SHOULD NOMINATE 2 NOMINEES, SUBMIT PROPOSE ORDER AND JUDGMENT AND IN THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR. THE PROPOSED ORDER SHOULD APPOINT A RECEIVER WITH LIMITED POWER AND THE FORM OF JUDGMENT SHOULD BE REDUCED TO THE PARTIES JANUARY 2007 STIPULATION. JUDGMENT SHOULD INDICATE THAT THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION FOR HEARING THE JUDGMENT IN DUE EFFECT. IF THERE ARE ADDITIONAL ISSUES THAT THE RECEIVER CANNOT RESOLVE, THE PARTIES CAN COME BACK TO COURT. (FOR DETAILS REVIEW MINI MINUTES) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Ntc Of Mtn To Enforce Settlement, To Enter Interlocutory Judgment On Settlement, For Appt Of Receiver & Issuing Of Restrining Orders
Off Calendar Mar-20-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. ALLAN SCHWARTZ, TEL. NO. 956-0712 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID D. STEIN, TEL. NO. 399-8824 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BARON MILLER, TEL. NO. 522-0500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SALVATORE CORTARA.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF SALVATORE SARINELLI'S MOTION To Enforce Settlement, To Enter Interlocutory Judgment On Settlement, For Appointment Of Receiver & Issuing Of Restraining Orders - THE COURT GAVE COUNSELS AN OPPORTUNITY TO DISCUSS AND SETTLE THE MATTER AMONG THEMSELVES OUTSIDE THE COURTROOM. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER, BOTH COUNSELS RECITED ON THE RECORD ISSUES THEY HAVE AGREED UPON INCLUDING THOSE NOT IN AGREEMENT. THE COURT INTERPRETED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT INCLUDING THE ISSUE WITH RESPECT TO THE CAFE AND LIQUOR LICENSE. PARTIES ARE AGREEABLE TO THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECIEVER WITH LIMITED POWER. THE COURT MADE THE FOLLOWING RULING: THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER UNTIL MARCH 24, 2007. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE BY MARCH 27, 2008, BY A JOINT LETTER TO THE COURT, EACH PARTY SHOULD NOMINATE TO THE COURT 2 NOMINEES WITH THE NAME OF RECEIVER AND THEIR REPECTIVE QUALIFICATION TO SERVE. COUNSELS TO SUBMIT PROPOSE ORDER AND JUDGMENT AND CAN BE DONE IN THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR. THE PROPOSED ORDER SHOULD APPOINT A RECEIVER WITH LIMITED POWER AND THE FORM OF JUDGMENT SHOULD BE REDUCED TO THE PARTIES JANUARY 2007 STIPULATION. JUDGMENT SHOULD INDICATE THAT THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION FOR HEARING THE JUDGMENT IN DUE EFFECT. IF THERE ARE ADDITIONAL ISSUES THAT THE RECEIVER CANNOT RESOLVE, THE PARTIES CAN COME BACK TO COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 301/PJB/PM

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Salvatore Sarinelli's Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. As Defendant has agreed to produce documents responsive to Nos. 1-7, 9, 11, 14, 23-25, and 28, Defendant is ordered to produce the responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order.
The motion is denied as to No. 8.
The motion is granted as to No. 10.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 12, 13, 16, 17, 18, 20, 21, 26, 27, and 29 as they are overly broad.
The motion is denied as to No. 15.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Mar-16-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Christine Chen, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Baron Miller, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as Modified: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 1 and 2. The response is incomplete as it does not respond with particularity. If the response may be made through a production of documents pursuant to C.C.P. Sections 2030.210(a)(2) and 2030.230, the response may be adequate.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7. As the response is made pursuant to If the response may be made through a production of documents pursuant to C.C.P. Sections 2030.210(a)(2) and 2030.230, the responses are adequate.
The motion is denied as to No. 8.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 12, 13, 14, and 20 as overly broad.
The motion is denied as to No. 14.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.

GRANTED as to Nos. 13 and 19
